project.title: BPC-157 Evidence Library
project.subtitle: History, human studies, and the current state of the evidence
project.description: A public-facing evidence library tracking BPC-157 sources, research summaries, and the strength of the documented literature.

researchQuestion: What is the documented history of BPC-157, what real studies exist, and how strong is the human evidence relative to the preclinical literature?
keyFindings: The BPC-157 literature begins in the 1990s and is heavily concentrated around a small set of recurring research groups, especially work linked to Zagreb/Croatia.
There is a large preclinical literature in rodents and cell systems, but published human evidence remains very limited.
A 2025 systematic review in orthopaedic sports medicine found 36 included studies from 1993 to 2024, of which 35 were preclinical and 1 was a clinical study.
The clearest publicly visible human records are a small retrospective knee-pain study, a two-person intravenous safety pilot, and a registered 2015 phase I oral trial record without posted results.
Official sources continue to treat BPC-157 as unapproved for human clinical use, and anti-doping bodies treat it as prohibited for athletes.

librarianWorkflow.goal: Locate primary sources first and separate them from commentary.
steps: Start with PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov, FDA, USADA/WADA, and patent databases.
Prefer original studies, registry records, official safety notices, and systematic reviews.
Flag repeated author groups so later summaries do not overstate independence of the evidence base.

librarianWorkflow.goal: Capture enough metadata that a future agent can reason without re-reading every source.
steps: Store each source in data/library.json with study type, population, evidence tier, and a plain-language takeaway.
Create a matching note in research/source-notes/ when the source materially affects the project summary.
Keep URLs pointed at stable primary locations such as PubMed, PMCID, ClinicalTrials.gov, FDA, or official organization pages.

librarianWorkflow.goal: Make the collection sortable by evidence quality, topic, and research role.
steps: Tag sources as history, human, preclinical, mechanistic, review, registry, or official-guidance.
Record study design explicitly instead of inferring strength from the title.
Separate findings from claims: note when a paper reports a result versus when it only cites earlier unpublished trials.

librarianWorkflow.goal: Support quick reuse by humans, agents, and the public site.
steps: Use npm run search -- <term> for terminal retrieval.
Use the GitHub Pages dashboard for public browsing by tag and evidence tier.
Use memory/START_HERE.md and memory/session-handoff.md to bootstrap a new research thread.

agentMemory.mission: Maintain a cautious, source-driven BPC-157 evidence library that distinguishes history, preclinical findings, human data, and official regulatory context.
startupChecklist: Check memory/session-handoff.md for last completed work and open questions.
Review data/library.json before adding or editing any summary claim.
Prefer updating source notes and reports before changing public-facing language.

reports.title: BPC-157: history and evidence snapshot
reports.summary: A short public-facing report covering the origin of the literature, where the human evidence exists, and why the current evidence base still remains weak for clinical claims.

reports.title: BPC-157: historical perspective and source inventory
reports.summary: A summary report that ties the historical development of BPC-157 research to the actual source categories currently documented in the repository.

reports.title: Librarian workflow and memory system
reports.summary: The operating structure for source finding, storage, cataloging, retrieval, and agent handoff so a fresh thread can continue the project without losing context.

sources.title: BPC 157's effect on healing
sources.authors: Seiwerth S, Sikiric P, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: One of the earliest indexed BPC-157 healing papers, reporting improved granulation tissue, collagen, angiogenesis, and tensile strength in rat models.
sources.limitations: Animal-only data from an early paper; not direct evidence of human benefit.

sources.title: Stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157 in trials for inflammatory bowel disease (PL-10, PLD-116, PL14736, Pliva, Croatia) heals ileoileal anastomosis in the rat
sources.authors: Vuksic T, Zoricic I, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Animal paper that also states BPC-157 had been safe in inflammatory bowel disease trials, making it an important bridge between preclinical claims and the thin human-trial record.
sources.limitations: The article cites clinical-trial safety but does not publish human trial outcomes itself.

sources.title: PCO-02 - Safety and Pharmacokinetics Trial
sources.authors: PharmaCotherapia d.o.o.
sources.studyDesign: Phase I randomized placebo-controlled pilot study
sources.keyTakeaway: Official registry record for an oral phase I safety/pharmacokinetic study of BPC-157 in healthy volunteers; the record shows unknown status and no posted results.
sources.limitations: Registration is not the same as published results; the record does not establish efficacy.

sources.title: Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 (PL 14736) improves ligament healing in the rat
sources.authors: Cerovecki T, Bojanic I, et al.
sources.population: Rat medial collateral ligament transection model
sources.keyTakeaway: Frequently cited animal study reporting improved ligament-healing measures across intraperitoneal, oral, and topical administration routes.
sources.limitations: Rodent model only; does not address clinical effectiveness in humans.

sources.title: The promoting effect of pentadecapeptide BPC 157 on tendon healing involves tendon outgrowth, cell survival, and cell migration
sources.authors: Chang CH, Tsai WC, Lin MS, Hsu YH, Pang JH
sources.population: Rat tendon explants and tendon fibroblast cultures
sources.keyTakeaway: Mechanistic study linking BPC-157 exposure to tendon fibroblast migration, survival under oxidative stress, and FAK/paxillin pathway activation.
sources.limitations: Mechanistic and ex vivo data are useful for plausibility but do not establish patient benefit.

sources.title: Intra-Articular Injection of BPC 157 for Multiple Types of Knee Pain
sources.authors: Lee E, Padgett B
sources.journal: Alternative Therapies in Health and Medicine
sources.keyTakeaway: Small retrospective clinic report suggesting symptom relief after intra-articular BPC-157 injections for varied knee-pain diagnoses.
sources.limitations: Very small uncontrolled study with phone follow-up, variable treatment, and no validated outcome instruments.

sources.title: Safety of Intravenous Infusion of BPC157 in Humans: A Pilot Study
sources.authors: Lee E, Burgess K
sources.journal: Alternative Therapies in Health and Medicine
sources.population: 2 healthy adults at a private clinic
sources.keyTakeaway: Published pilot safety study reporting no measured adverse effects after one-hour intravenous infusions up to 20 mg in two adults.
sources.limitations: Sample size of two and prior exposure in both participants sharply limits what can be inferred about safety.

sources.title: Multifunctionality and Possible Medical Application of the BPC 157 Peptide-Literature and Patent Review
sources.authors: Jozwiak M, Bauer M, Kamysz W, Kleczkowska P
sources.keyTakeaway: Recent open-access review summarizing the broad literature and patent activity while explicitly acknowledging the absence of sufficient human clinical evidence for approval.
sources.limitations: A review synthesizes prior work and inherits the quality problems of the underlying literature.

sources.title: Emerging Use of BPC-157 in Orthopaedic Sports Medicine: A Systematic Review
sources.authors: Vasireddi N, Hahamyan H, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Important current synthesis: 35 included studies were preclinical and only 1 was clinical, underscoring how thin the human evidence base still is.
sources.limitations: The review is limited by the underlying evidence base, which is mostly lower-level preclinical work.

sources.title: Therapeutic Peptides in Orthopaedics: Applications, Challenges, and Future Directions
sources.authors: Rahman OF, Lee SJ, Seeds WA
sources.journal: Journal of the American Academy of Orthopaedic Surgeons Global Research & Reviews
sources.keyTakeaway: Recent orthopaedic review notes that therapeutic peptides, including BPC-157, remain promising preclinically but lack clinical-trial support.
sources.limitations: Broad review rather than a dedicated BPC-157 clinical assessment.

sources.title: BPC-157: Experimental Peptide Creates Risk for Athletes
sources.authors: USADA
sources.keyTakeaway: USADA states BPC-157 is prohibited under the WADA Prohibited List category S0 Unapproved Substances and is not approved for human clinical use.
sources.limitations: Guidance document, not a clinical study.

sources.title: Safety Risks Associated with Certain Bulk Drug Substances Nominated for Use in Compounding: BPC-157 entry
sources.authors: FDA
sources.keyTakeaway: FDA's compounding safety-risk page flags BPC-157 for immunogenicity concerns, peptide-related impurity complexity, and nonclinical findings that may include reproductive or protumorigenic risk signals.
sources.limitations: Regulatory safety summary, not a direct clinical-trial report.

sources.title: A new gastric juice peptide, BPC. An overview of the stomach-stress-organoprotection hypothesis and beneficial effects of BPC.
sources.authors: Sikiric P, Petek M, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Foundational early review framing BPC as part of the stomach-stress-organoprotection hypothesis before the later BPC-157 literature expanded.
sources.limitations: Historical orientation source rather than direct modern efficacy evidence.

sources.title: Gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157 accelerates healing of transected rat Achilles tendon and in vitro stimulates tendocytes growth.
sources.authors: Staresinic M, Sebecic B, et al.
sources.studyDesign: Preclinical animal and in vitro study
sources.population: Rat Achilles tendon transection model and cultured tendocytes
sources.keyTakeaway: Early musculoskeletal paper showing accelerated Achilles tendon healing in rats together with tendocyte growth effects in vitro.
sources.limitations: Preclinical work only; not direct human evidence.

sources.title: BPC 157 and blood vessels.
sources.authors: Seiwerth S, Brcic L, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Focused review on vascular and endothelial themes that recur across BPC-157 mechanistic claims.
sources.limitations: Narrative review built mostly on preclinical literature.

sources.title: Stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157 in the treatment of colitis and ischemia and reperfusion in rats: New insights.
sources.authors: Duzel A, Vlainic J, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Detailed gastrointestinal preclinical paper connecting BPC-157 to colitis and ischemia-reperfusion models.
sources.limitations: Animal data only, despite therapeutic framing.

sources.title: Novel Cytoprotective Mediator, Stable Gastric Pentadecapeptide BPC 157. Vascular Recruitment and Gastrointestinal Tract Healing.
sources.authors: Sikiric P, Rucman R, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Review linking cytoprotection, vascular recruitment, and gastrointestinal healing themes across the BPC-157 literature.
sources.limitations: Review article rather than new experimental evidence.

sources.title: BPC 157 and Standard Angiogenic Growth Factors. Gastrointestinal Tract Healing, Lessons from Tendon, Ligament, Muscle and Bone Healing.
sources.authors: Seiwerth S, Rucman R, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Integrative review linking angiogenic growth factors and gastrointestinal healing with tendon, ligament, muscle, and bone-repair themes in the BPC-157 literature.
sources.limitations: Review-level synthesis rather than new controlled evidence.

sources.title: Gastric pentadecapeptide body protection compound BPC 157 and its role in accelerating musculoskeletal soft tissue healing.
sources.authors: Gwyer D, Wragg NM, Wilson SL
sources.keyTakeaway: Independent musculoskeletal review summarizing why BPC-157 became associated with soft-tissue healing claims.
sources.limitations: Review-level evidence and still heavily dependent on preclinical studies.

sources.title: Stable Gastric Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 and Wound Healing.
sources.authors: Seiwerth S, Milavic M, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Broad wound-healing review summarizing how BPC-157 has been positioned across repair models.
sources.limitations: Synthesis source rather than new controlled clinical evidence.

sources.title: BPC 157 Rescued NSAID-cytotoxicity Via Stabilizing Intestinal Permeability and Enhancing Cytoprotection.
sources.authors: Park JM, Lee HJ, Sikiric P, Hahm KB
sources.keyTakeaway: Focused paper on BPC-157 in NSAID-related cytotoxicity and intestinal-permeability/cytoprotection themes.
sources.limitations: Useful for mechanism framing, but not direct clinical proof in humans.

sources.title: Stable Gastric Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 and Striated, Smooth, and Heart Muscle.
sources.authors: Staresinic M, Japjec M, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Systems-oriented review focusing on BPC-157 claims across striated, smooth, and cardiac muscle contexts.
sources.limitations: Primarily a synthesis of preclinical findings from a recurring research network.

sources.title: Pharmacokinetics, distribution, metabolism, and excretion of body-protective compound 157, a potential drug for treating various wounds, in rats and dogs.
sources.authors: He L, Feng D, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Pharmacokinetic and metabolism paper addressing distribution, metabolism, and excretion in animal models rather than efficacy.
sources.limitations: Useful for drug-handling questions, but still preclinical and not human PK.

sources.title: The Stable Gastric Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 Pleiotropic Beneficial Activity and Its Possible Relations with Neurotransmitter Activity.
sources.authors: Sikiric P, Boban Blagaic A, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Recent review extending BPC-157 claims into neurotransmitter and neuropharmacology-related mechanisms.
sources.limitations: Mechanistic review that does not resolve the underlying clinical-evidence gap.

sources.title: Doxorubicine-congestive heart failure-increased big endothelin-1 plasma concentration: reversal by amlodipine, losartan, and gastric pentadecapeptide BPC157 in rat and mouse.
sources.authors: Lovric-Bencic M, Sikiric P, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Animal study extending BPC-157 claims into doxorubicin-associated heart-failure and endothelin-related vascular injury contexts.
sources.limitations: Preclinical cardiovascular model only; not human cardiac evidence.

sources.title: Effects of pentadecapeptide BPC157 on regional serotonin synthesis in the rat brain: alpha-methyl-L-tryptophan autoradiographic measurements.
sources.authors: Tohyama Y, Sikiric P, Diksic M
sources.keyTakeaway: Direct BPC-157 paper examining regional serotonin-synthesis effects in rat brain, relevant to later neurotransmitter-focused reviews.
sources.limitations: Animal neurochemistry study rather than clinical neuropsychiatric evidence.

sources.title: Achilles detachment in rat and stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157: Promoted tendon-to-bone healing and opposed corticosteroid aggravation.
sources.authors: Krivic A, Anic T, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Musculoskeletal animal study on tendon-to-bone healing and corticosteroid-aggravated injury, commonly relevant to repair claims around BPC-157.
sources.limitations: Rat model only; no direct translation to clinical tendon repair.

sources.title: Stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157 and bupivacaine.
sources.authors: Zivanovic-Posilovic G, Balenovic D, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Direct BPC-157 paper examining interactions in a bupivacaine-related injury/pharmacology context.
sources.limitations: Specialized preclinical model, not direct evidence for human use with local anesthetics.

sources.title: Esophagogastric anastomosis in rats: Improved healing by BPC 157 and L-arginine, aggravated by L-NAME.
sources.authors: Djakovic Z, Djakovic I, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Gastrointestinal repair paper linking BPC-157 effects with nitric-oxide pathway modifiers in a rat anastomosis model.
sources.limitations: Animal surgery model only; does not establish human postoperative benefit.

sources.title: BPC157 as Potential Agent Rescuing from Cancer Cachexia.
sources.authors: Kang EA, Han YM, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Focused paper extending BPC-157 claims into cancer-cachexia and systemic catabolic-injury contexts.
sources.limitations: Disease-area review framing rather than direct clinical cancer evidence.

sources.title: An endogeous defensive concept, renewed cytoprotection/adaptive cytoprotection: intra(per)-oral/intragastric strong alcohol in rat. Involvement of pentadecapeptide BPC 157 and nitric oxide system.
sources.authors: Becejac T, Cesarec V, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Preclinical paper connecting BPC-157 to cytoprotection/adaptive cytoprotection and nitric-oxide-system themes in alcohol injury models.
sources.limitations: Mechanistic animal study only.

sources.title: Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 resolves suprahepatic occlusion of the inferior caval vein, Budd-Chiari syndrome model in rats.
sources.authors: Gojkovic S, Krezic I, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Direct BPC-157 paper extending vascular/hemodynamic claims into a Budd-Chiari syndrome rat model.
sources.limitations: Specialized animal disease model with no direct human clinical evidence.

sources.title: Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 resolves Pringle maneuver in rats, both ischemia and reperfusion.
sources.authors: Kolovrat M, Gojkovic S, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Preclinical liver paper on BPC-157 in ischemia-reperfusion injury during the Pringle maneuver model.
sources.limitations: Animal-model evidence only.

sources.title: Stable Gastric Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 Heals Established Vesicovaginal Fistula and Counteracts Stone Formation in Rats.
sources.authors: Rasic D, Zenko Sever A, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Fistula-healing animal study that broadens the repair literature beyond musculoskeletal and GI models.
sources.limitations: Animal fistula model only; not human reconstructive evidence.

sources.title: Stable Gastric Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 as a Therapy for the Disable Myotendinous Junctions in Rats.
sources.authors: Japjec M, Horvat Pavlov K, et al.
sources.keyTakeaway: Direct musculoskeletal paper on BPC-157 in disabled myotendinous junctions, relevant to later repair-oriented reviews.
sources.limitations: Animal injury model only.

sources.title: BPC 157, L-NAME, L-Arginine, NO-Relation, in the Suited Rat Ketamine Models Resembling "Negative-Like" Symptoms of Schizophrenia.
sources.authors: Zemba Cilic A, Zemba M, et al.
sources.population: Rat ketamine model of negative-like symptoms
sources.keyTakeaway: Neurobehavioral animal paper linking BPC-157 to ketamine-model negative-like symptoms and nitric-oxide-system framing.
sources.limitations: Highly model-dependent preclinical neuropsychiatric evidence.
